narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've poured into your dream of becoming a lawyer, and I want to walk you through why our predictive model saw your path to passing the bar exam as riskier than we'd hoped—landing at a 63% chance of failure that tipped the scales against your application. Picture it like a story the model pieced together from your profile: your Year 3 decile of 1.0 stood out as the biggest chapter, signaling a steep drop in performance that echoed struggles for others in similar spots. Layered on that was your LSAT score of 32, which the model saw as another strong pull toward challenges ahead, like a headwind that many with that mark couldn't fully overcome. Your Year 1 decile of 3.0 added to this narrative of inconsistency early on, painting a picture of grades that didn't build the momentum needed for bar success. Coming from a lower-middle family income bracket factored in too, as it often correlates with fewer resources to bridge those gaps for students like you. Your background as a Black female full-time student wove in subtly but noticeably, reflecting patterns the model has seen where systemic hurdles make the climb tougher. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.2, while a bright spot, couldn't outweigh these combined forces tugging toward failure. It's not about your potential or heart—it's this specific blend that made the model cautious, like warning of storm clouds on an otherwise promising horizon. You've got strengths here that shine through, and with targeted steps like boosting those deciles or prepping intensely for the bar, you could rewrite this story. We're rooting for you to prove it wrong and chase that law dream fiercely. Keep pushing forward.
